publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2021-04-12 18:34:52 UTC.

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.unittitle: David Scheinbaum hip hop photographs
did.abstract: Several hundred photographs of Hip Hop artists and musicians, taken primarily during their performances in Albuquerque, NM between 2001 and 2012, along with some additional shows at venues in Oakland and Los Angeles, California.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: David Scheinbaum is former Director/Chair of the Photography Department at the Santa Fe University of Art and Design, and Professor Emeritus, College of Santa Fe. He worked with the preeminent photo historian Beaumont Newhall from 1978 until Newhall's death in 1993 and continues as co-executor of his estate. With his wife, Janet Russek, he operates Scheinbaum & Russek Ltd., private fine art photography dealers and consultants in Santa Fe, New Mexico and exclusively represents the estates of Beaumont and Nancy Newhall, and Eliot Porter. His photographic projects and publications include Miami Beach: Photographs of an American Dream (Florida International University Press, 1990), Bisti (UNM Press, 1987), Stone: A Substantial Witness (Museum of New Mexico Press, 2006) and Images in the Heavens, Patterns on the Earth: The I Ching (The Museum of New Mexico Press, 2005) (with Janet Russek)

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: Several hundred photographs of Hip Hop artists and musicians, taken primarily during their performances in Albuquerque, NM between 2001 and 2012, along with some additional shows at venues in Oakland and Los Angeles, California. The photographs depict dozens of Hip Hop artists, both on stage and behind the scenes, in formats ranging between 24x36" color exhibition prints to 8x10" b/w work prints. Artists and groups include Jurassic 5, Gang Starr, The Pharcyde, MF Doom, Common, KRS-One, Nas, De La Soul, Ozomotli, Mos Def, Black Star, Talib Kweli, Erykah Badu, The Roots, Public Enemy, Dialated Peoples, Atmosphere, Blackalicious, Mr. Lif, Jean Grae, Ice Cube, Wu Tang Clan, Snoop Dog, George Clinton, Hieroglyphics, Cut Chemist, Beat Junkies, and many more.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: David Scheinbaum hip hop photographs, #8137. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 11 restricted to the permission of the curator.

did.unittitle: Exhibition prints
did.physdesc: Matted 11 x 14 exhibition prints, printed in 2002. Prints in box 12 are not matted, and were printed in 2006.

did.unittitle: People Under the Stairs, Thes One, Sunshine Theater, Albuquerque, NM
did.physdesc: Mounted, signed, titled and dated in pencil on the mount, the photographer's stamp on the reverse, signed in pencil on the windowmat.

did.unittitle: Color exhibition prints
did.physdesc: Each photograph has a label with the photographer's credit on the verso. Color exhibition prints, not matted, printed in 2011-2013, except for those marked individually with a 2019 print date.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: Access to box 11 restricted to the permission of the curator.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Eight of these prints were exhibited at the Norton Museum of Art in 2012.

Work prints

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: An item level spreadsheet of the work prints is available upon request. Contains additonal dates and locations for a few of the work prints.

did.unittitle: Black Star, Phar Cyde, Cody Chesnutt, Sina Sao, Erykah Badu, Talib Kweli, Eyedea & Abilities, Toure, Mos Def, X-Ecutioners, Ozomotli, Zion I
did.physdesc: Almost all titled in ink in the margin.
